Text: The proposed budget for the upcoming renovation project is consistent {{c1::with}} the estimates provided by the architectural firm.
Why: consistent 固定搭配 with（與…一致）；常見錯選 to — 學習者常受 conform to 影響而誤用 to，但形容詞 consistent 慣用介係詞為 with。
Example: The manager confirmed that the employee's performance review was consistent with her outstanding sales records this year.

Text: Employees who have completed three years of continuous service are typically eligible {{c1::for}} the company's comprehensive tuition reimbursement program.
Why: eligible 固定搭配介係詞 for 表示「具備...的資格」；常見錯選 to — 學習者常混淆 eligible to (後接動詞) 與 eligible for (後接名詞) 的用法。
Example: Only permanent staff members are eligible for the performance-based bonuses distributed at the end of the fiscal year.

Text: All contractors are strictly required to comply {{c1::with}} the safety regulations outlined in the legal agreement.
Why: comply 為不及物動詞，固定搭配介系詞 with 表示「遵守」；常見錯選 to — 學習者常受 conform to 或 adhere to 的干擾，但 comply 習慣上只接 with。
Example: The manufacturing facility underwent a thorough inspection to ensure it continues to comply with international environmental standards.

Text: The project manager is responsible {{c1::for}} overseeing the entire recruitment process for the upcoming summer internship program.
Why: responsible 固定搭配介係詞 for 表負責；常見錯選 of — 受到中文「對...負責」字面直譯陷阱，但 responsible of 在英語中並不正確。
Example: Each team member is responsible for submitting their expense reports to the accounting department by Friday.

Text: Please ensure that you {{c1::follow up}} on the outstanding invoices before the accounting department closes for the weekend.
Why: follow up on 表示針對某事採取後續行動以求進展；常見錯選 follow up with — with 後通常接人（與某人聯繫），on 則接具體事項（處理進度），此處對象是發票應選 on。
Example: I will call the human resources manager this afternoon to follow up on my job application status.

Text: Customers who {{c1::place}} an order before noon are eligible for same-day delivery on all office supplies.
Why: place an order 為固定動詞短語，表示「下訂單」；常見錯選 make — 受到中文「做」訂單的字面對譯影響，但在正式商務英語中，下訂單標準動詞為 place。
Example: Please contact our procurement department directly if you wish to place an order for the new laboratory equipment.

Text: The IT department will {{c1::set up}} the new workstations in the conference room before the orientation begins tomorrow morning.
Why: set up 是商務常見片語動詞，意為「架設」或「安排」；常見錯選 set in — set in 指（負面情況）開始並持續，如寒流或雨季開始，不符合在此處安裝硬體設備的語境。
Example: The company plans to set up several new branches across Southeast Asia by the end of next year.

Text: The research department is scheduled to {{c1::carry out}} a comprehensive market survey to identify potential consumer trends for next year.
Why: carry out 表示「執行、進行」（如計畫或研究）; 常見錯選 carry on — 後者意為「繼續」，不符合此處需完成特定任務的語境。
Example: To ensure the highest quality standards, our quality control team will carry out regular inspections of the manufacturing facility.

Text: The management team promised to {{c1::look into}} the recent complaints regarding the delayed shipment of office supplies.
Why: look into 意為「調查」或「研究」，常用於深入了解問題或提案；常見錯選 look for — look for 是指「尋找」遺失或特定的對象，而非「調查」事情原委。
Example: Our IT department will look into the possibility of upgrading our servers to improve website performance next month.

Text: After three days of intensive negotiations, the board members finally managed to {{c1::reach}} a consensus on the proposed merger.
Why: reach a consensus 為固定動詞短語，表示「達成共識」；常見錯選 make — "make a consensus" 不符合道地搭配，consensus 通常與 reach 或 achieve 連用。
Example: The committee is expected to reach a consensus regarding the new environmental guidelines by the end of this month.

Text: The department manager has requested that all project leads {{c1::attend}} a meeting next Tuesday to discuss the upcoming budget cuts.
Why: attend 為及物動詞，意為「出席」，後方直接加受詞；常見錯選 join — join 通常指「加入」組織或群體，而出席正式會議應使用 attend。
Example: If you are unable to attend a meeting in person, you can join the discussion via video conference.

Text: {{c1::Despite}} the short notice, the public relations department successfully coordinated a press conference for the CEO's announcement.
Why: Despite 是介係詞，後接名詞片語（the short notice）來表示讓步；常見錯選 Although — Although 是連接詞，後面必須引導包含主詞與動詞的完整子句，不可直接接名詞。
Example: The logistics team ensured that all shipments arrived on schedule despite the severe weather conditions at the port.

Text: {{c1::Although}} the marketing campaign was expensive, it successfully generated a significant amount of interest from potential investors.
Why: Although 是連指詞（Conjunction），用以引導讓步子句（含主語與動詞）；常見錯選 Despite — Despite 是介係詞，後方僅能接名詞或動名詞，不可直接接完整的子句結構。
Example: The technician managed to fix the printer quickly, although the replacement parts were delayed by several days.

Text: By the time the new supervisor arrives next month, the team {{c1::will have completed}} the entire warehouse inventory audit.
Why: 此處應使用未來完成式，表示在未來特定時間點（下個月）之前將已完成的動作；常見錯選 will complete — 一般未來式僅表示將要發生的動作，缺乏在特定時間點前「完成」的語境。
Example: The accounting department will have completed the annual financial review by the time the board meeting begins tomorrow.

Text: To finish the project on time, our department must work overtime to {{c1::meet}} a deadline set by the CEO.
Why: meet 與 deadline 為固定動詞短語搭配，意為「趕上截止日期」；常見錯選 catch — catch a deadline 是常見的直譯錯誤，道地商用英文固定使用 meet。
Example: The marketing agency requested an extension because they were unable to meet a deadline for the ad campaign.

Text: Increased operational costs account {{c1::for}} the significant decrease in our net profit margin this fiscal year.
Why: account for 為固定搭配，指「解釋」或「佔（比例）」；常見錯選 of — 學習者常受中文「佔...的比例」影響而誤用 of，但動詞用法中 account 必接 for。
Example: Travel expenses and client dinners account for nearly twenty percent of the department's annual budget.

Text: A failure to address the client's concerns promptly could result {{c1::in}} the termination of our long-term service contract.
Why: result 在表達「造成、導致」結果時必接介系詞 in；常見錯選 from — result from 表示「起因於」，在此情境下會使因果關係顛倒。
Example: The implementation of the new efficiency measures will hopefully result in reduced overhead costs for the logistics department.

Text: The survival of the small tech startup will depend {{c1::on}} whether they can secure a second round of venture capital funding.
Why: depend 固定搭配介系詞 on 表示「取決於」或「依賴」；常見錯選 to — 這是受 adapt to 或 conform to 等常見動詞搭配影響的典型介系詞混淆錯誤。
Example: Logistics managers often depend on real-time tracking data to ensure that shipments arrive at the warehouse on schedule.

Text: The newly proposed budget for the marketing department will consist {{c1::of}} several allocations for digital advertising and public relations.
Why: consist 為不及物動詞，固定搭配 of 表示由哪些要素組成；常見錯選 with — 學習者常受 consistent with 的搭配干擾，但動詞 consist 必接 of。
Example: The screening process for the senior executive position will consist of multiple rounds of interviews and a case study.

Text: Small business owners can benefit {{c1::from}} the recent tax incentives introduced to encourage local investment.
Why: benefit 作為動詞時，後接 from 以表示從中獲益；常見錯選 of — "benefit of" 是名詞用法（...的好處），在此處動詞句型中應使用 from。
Example: Our marketing department will certainly benefit from your extensive experience in social media advertising.

Text: Employees are encouraged to engage {{c1::in}} professional development workshops to enhance their leadership skills within the organization.
Why: engage 為不及物動詞，後接 in 表示從事或參與某項活動；常見錯選 with — engage with 常用於與人互動，而從事特定商務活動或行為須用 in。
Example: The marketing department decided to engage in a new social media campaign to reach a younger demographic.

Text: Our firm continues to specialize {{c1::in}} providing customized logistics solutions for multinational manufacturing corporations operating in Southeast Asia.
Why: specialize 為不及物動詞，固定搭配介系詞 in 表示「專精於某領域」；常見錯選 at — 學習者常誤用 at 來表示地點或點狀目標，但專長領域須用 in。
Example: The local boutique law practice decided to specialize in intellectual property rights to differentiate itself from larger competitors.

Text: The marketing director made sure that everyone in the office was aware {{c1::of}} the changes to the project deadline.
Why: aware 為形容詞，固定搭配介系詞 of 表示「意識到」或「知道」；常見錯選 about — 受中文「對...知道/了解」影響，導致誤用 aware about，但標準用法僅接 of。
Example: Investors should be fully aware of the potential risks before committing capital to a new startup venture.

Text: New hires are encouraged to become familiar {{c1::with}} the corporate travel policy before booking any international flights.
Why: familiar 固定搭配介係詞 with，表示「熟悉…」；常見錯選 of — 這是受到 familiarity of 或中文「對…的熟悉」字面直譯的干擾，但在形容詞用法下必須用 with。
Example: The technical support team is already familiar with the software bugs reported in the latest version of the application.

Text: Employees are kindly requested to refrain {{c1::from}} using personal mobile devices during the quarterly board meeting to ensure full focus.
Why: refrain 固定搭配介系詞 from 表示「克制/避免做某事」；常見錯選 to — 學習者常受不定詞 (refrain to do) 的句型誤導，但該字後方必接 from + V-ing。
Example: Visitors must refrain from taking photographs inside the gallery while the temporary exhibition is on display.

Text: Contrary {{c1::to}} the initial feedback from the marketing department, the client actually approved the proposed advertising campaign.
Why: contrary 固定搭配介系詞 to，用來表示「與…相反」；常見錯選 with — 學習者常受 contrast with 或 compare with 影響，但 contrary 慣用法是接 to。
Example: The company's stock price rose sharply this morning, contrary to the pessimistic predictions made by several financial analysts.

Text: All contractual agreements are subject {{c1::to}} final approval by the legal department before any payments can be processed.
Why: subject to 為固定形容詞片語，表示「取決於」或「受制於」，其中 to 是介係詞；常見錯選 of — 學習者常受 subject of（…的主題）干擾，但表達條件限制時必接 to。
Example: Shipping dates for overseas orders are subject to change depending on customs clearance and local port conditions.

Text: Employees are required to {{c1::hand in}} their weekly progress reports to the department manager every Friday afternoon.
Why: hand in 意為「交上、遞交」（與 submit 同義）；常見錯選 hand out — hand out 是「分發」之意，邏輯與「向主管提交報告」相反，是常見的動詞片語混淆。
Example: After receiving a better job offer from a competitor, the senior developer decided to hand in his resignation notice today.

Text: I do not have the specific sales figures right now, but I will {{c1::get back to}} you once the meeting concludes.
Why: get back to 為商務通訊常用片語，表示「（查明後）再回覆某人」；常見錯選 return to — return to 多指「回到某處」或「恢復某狀態」，不適用於回覆訊息的語境。
Example: Please allow our support team some time to investigate the issue, and we will get back to you shortly.

Text: During the monthly board meeting, the marketing director decided to {{c1::bring up}} the issue of the recent drop in sales figures.
Why: bring up 意為「在討論中提及或提出某話題」；常見錯選 bring — bring 若單獨使用僅表示「攜帶」或「帶來」，在商務會議脈絡下，必須搭配 up 才能表達「主動提出議題」的意思。
Example: If you have any concerns regarding the new safety guidelines, please bring up the matter with your supervisor as soon as possible.

Text: Due to unexpected technical difficulties, the IT department had to {{c1::put off}} the scheduled system maintenance until tomorrow morning.
Why: put off 意為「推遲、延期」，用於改動預定行程；常見錯選 call off — 兩者皆有延後之意，但 call off 指的是完全「取消」，不符合後文改至明早的語境。
Example: The board of directors agreed to put off the final vote on the budget until more data is available.

Text: After the CEO retires next month, the current marketing director will {{c1::take over}} the daily operations of the regional branch.
Why: take over 表示「接管」職責或公司；常見錯選 hand over — hand over 是「交出」職責，語意方向與 take over 的「接手」完全相反。
Example: Global Logistics Inc. announced plans to take over its main competitor to expand its market share in Europe.

Text: The marketing department needs to {{c1::come up with}} a creative advertising campaign to boost sales before the product launch.
Why: come up with 表示「想出、提出（主意或方案）」；常見錯選 keep up with — 後者意為「跟上進度或趨勢」，並不包含「創造或發想」新事物的含義。
Example: The management team had to come up with a contingency plan after the primary supplier announced a three-week delay.

Text: The logistics manager is concerned that the warehouse might {{c1::run out of}} packing tape before the next shipment arrives.
Why: run out of 表示用完或耗盡；常見錯選 run out — run out 自身是不及物動詞（如 Time is running out），若接受詞時則必須搭配介系詞 of。
Example: During the peak holiday season, many retail stores frequently run out of popular electronic gadgets within hours of opening.

Text: The board of directors will {{c1::make}} a decision regarding the merger after reviewing the final financial report next week.
Why: make a decision 為固定搭配，表示「下決定」；常見錯選 do — 受中文「做」決定的字面影響，學習者常直譯為 do，但英語中 decision 慣用的動詞為 make。
Example: It is often difficult for a project manager to make a decision without having access to all necessary data.

Text: The board of directors decided to {{c1::turn down}} the merger proposal because the valuation was significantly lower than initially expected.
Why: turn down 為「拒絕」之意，常用於拒絕提議、職缺或申請；常見錯選 turn off — 此為「關閉（電源）」之意，兩者動詞部分相同但語意完全不同。
Example: After reviewing the budget, the manager was forced to turn down the request for additional staff in the marketing department.

Text: If any team members feel that the current project timeline is unrealistic, they should {{c1::raise}} an issue during the next weekly meeting.
Why: raise 作為動詞在此意為「提出（議題、疑問）」，與 an issue 形成固定動詞搭配；常見錯選 rise — rise 是不及物動詞，表示上升、增加，後方不可直接加受詞。
Example: The safety inspector decided to raise an issue regarding the lack of proper ventilation in the factory warehouse.

Text: The department head was prepared to {{c1::take}} responsibility for the budget deficit reported in the last fiscal year.
Why: take 與 responsibility 為固定動詞搭配，意為「承擔責任」；常見錯選 do — 受中文「做、負責」的語法影響，學習者常誤用 do responsibility。
Example: Even when errors occur due to external factors, a good leader will take responsibility for their team's performance.

Text: The marketing department needs to {{c1::conduct}} research to understand the changing preferences of our young consumer base.
Why: conduct 是正式動詞，常用於 research 或 survey 等調查活動；常見錯選 make — 受中文「做研究」影響，但英語中 research 固定搭配 conduct 或 do。
Example: Pharmaceutical companies must conduct research for several years before a new drug can be approved for public use.

Text: The marketing department is planning to {{c1::launch}} a campaign to promote the new line of eco-friendly products next month.
Why: launch a campaign 是固定搭配，表示「發起行銷活動」；常見錯選 start — start 雖通順，但在正式商務情境中，launch 更常用於表達產品或計畫的正式發布。
Example: The startup decided to launch a campaign on social media to increase brand awareness among younger consumers.

Text: If your luggage is damaged upon arrival, you should {{c1::file}} a complaint at the customer service desk immediately.
Why: file a complaint 為正式提出投訴的固定搭配，常用於商務、法律或行政流程；常見錯選 fill — 學習者常將 file (提出) 與 fill (填寫) 混淆，但投訴行為應使用 file。
Example: After receiving the wrong items twice, the client decided to file a complaint against the shipping department.

Text: The marketing team's innovative social media campaign managed to {{c1::exceed}} expectations, resulting in a forty percent increase in new subscriptions.
Why: exceed expectations 為固定動詞搭配（collocation），意為「超出預期」；常見錯選 surpass — 雖然 surpass 亦有超越之意，但在多益職場情境中，針對 expectations（預期）或 performance（表現）最道地的動詞首選是 exceed。
Example: If the quarterly profits exceed expectations, all employees in the sales department will receive a performance-based bonus.

Text: After months of intense negotiations, our sales team finally managed to {{c1::secure}} a contract with the multinational corporation.
Why: secure 在商務情境指「成功獲取、簽下」合約，是常見的固定搭配；常見錯選 obtain — 雖然 obtain 也有獲得之意，但 secure 更有「在競爭中爭取並鎖定成果」的語氣。
Example: The startup was able to secure a contract for the infrastructure project despite stiff competition.

Text: The original proposal focused solely on cost reduction, {{c1::whereas}} the revised version emphasizes long-term growth and market expansion.
Why: whereas 用於正式對比兩組平行的事實（前後子句結構通常對稱）；常見錯選 although — although 主要引導「儘管」的讓步語氣，而 whereas 則強烈對照兩者性質的差異。
Example: Sales in the European region grew by ten percent, whereas the Asian market experienced a slight decline during the last quarter.

Text: The spokesperson acknowledged the recent technical difficulties; {{c1::nonetheless}}, the official product launch will proceed as scheduled next Tuesday.
Why: nonetheless 是副詞，用於轉折語氣表示「儘管如此」；常見錯選 although — although 是連接詞，後面必須直接連接子句，不能在分號後單獨作為轉折語使用。
Example: Customer demand for the legacy model decreased; nonetheless, the company will continue providing technical support services for five more years.

Text: The marketing team will not proceed with the revised advertising campaign {{c1::unless}} they receive final approval from the director.
Why: unless 是「除非」的意思，引導表示「唯一例外條件」的從句；常見錯選 if — 因為 if 表「如果」，在此否定句中若選 if 會導致邏輯相反（變成有批准就不執行）。
Example: The annual conference will be held outdoors next Tuesday unless it rains heavily throughout the morning.

Text: The director decided to proceed with the press conference {{c1::in spite of}} the technical difficulties with the video streaming equipment.
Why: in spite of 是介係詞片語，後接名詞或動名詞；常見錯選 although — although 是從屬連接詞，後方必須接包含主詞與動詞的完整子句。
Example: The marketing team successfully completed the client presentation in spite of having very little time to prepare the digital slides.

Text: The marketing team has decided to postpone the product launch {{c1::given}} that the final user feedback report has not been completed.
Why: given that 為固定連接詞，表示「考慮到」或「鑒於」；常見錯選 giving — 學習者常受分詞構句影響而誤用現在分詞，但 given that 是引導原因子句的專用詞組。
Example: The flight was delayed for two hours given that the maintenance crew needed more time to inspect the aircraft engine.

Text: Ms. Tanaka {{c1::has been working}} as the lead recruitment specialist since the human resources department was restructured last spring.
Why: 現在完成進行式強調從過去起點持續至今的動作，常搭配 since 使用；常見錯選 works — 現在式無法表達從過去持續至今的時態特徵。
Example: Our engineering team has been working on the software update for three months to ensure system stability.

Text: Had the department head checked his notifications earlier, he {{c1::would have known}} about the urgent conference call scheduled for this morning.
Why: 與過去事實相反的假設語氣（第三類條件句），主句須用 would have + p.p.；常見錯選 would know — 這是對現在假設的誤用，忽略了句首倒裝的過去完成式。
Example: The PR director would have known about the negative news if he had monitored the media channels over the weekend.
